articles.title: Received extremely severe alert on your phone: What is means and how this emergency notification system works
articles.description: India is testing its indigenous Cell Broadcasting system. This system sends emergency alerts to mobile phones during disasters. The test messages are being sent in English and Hindi. The system ensures quick dissemination of warnings for natural and man-made emergencies. It is operational across all states and union territories. Alerts are issued in over 19 Indian languages.

articles.title: How Hezbollah’s low-cost drones are reshaping the battlefield as Israel scrambles to adapt
articles.description: Hezbollah's deployment of cheap, fibre-optic drones is presenting a significant challenge to Israeli forces in southern Lebanon. These low-tech, untraceable devices, unlike GPS-guided ones, are difficult to counter, forcing the Israeli military to adapt its tactics. The group is manufacturing these drones domestically, exploiting perceived weaknesses in Israeli defenses.

articles.title: Inside the Musk–OpenAI trial: Key moments from the billion-dollar legal battle
articles.description: Elon Musk is suing OpenAI, alleging the AI company abandoned its nonprofit mission for profit. He claims he was misled about the shift to a commercial model, which he views as a "bait and switch." Musk seeks to return OpenAI to its original purpose and remove current leadership.

articles.title: Maharashtra HSC result 2026: Steps to check scorecards via DigiLocker, UMANG, SMS and official portals
articles.description: The Maharashtra State Board will release the HSC Result 2026 through multiple platforms, ensuring easy access for students. Results can be checked via official websites, DigiLocker, the UMANG app, and SMS services. DigiLocker allows secure access to digital marksheets using Aadhaar-linked login, while UMANG integrates government services for result viewing. Students must enter accurate credentials as per their hall tickets. Online marksheets are provisional and valid for admissions, with original certificates to be collected later from respective institutions.

articles.description: Spotify has started to verify non-AI artists, in a bid to increase "authenticity and trust for the artists behind the music". It's also a surefire way to imply that any artist who isn't verified is most likely AI. The Verified by Spotify badge will signify that the artist in question has consistent listener activity and engagement over time, a good standing with Spotify's platform policies, and "an identifiable artist presence both on and off-platform, like concert dates, merch, and linked social accounts".
